commit.message:
fix(ci): 避免 release 构建产生 dirty 版本元信息 (#17)

## Why
- 当前 release CI 会对 `.github/scripts/rust-link-wrapper.sh` 执行 `chmod
+x`，导致构建时 Git worktree 变 dirty。
- `shadow-rs` 会把这个 dirty 状态写进 `kat --version`，影响通过 `cargo binstall`
安装下来的预编译二进制。

## What
- 删除 CI 里构建前对 linker wrapper 的额外 `chmod +x` 步骤。
- 直接把 `.github/scripts/rust-link-wrapper.sh` 以可执行权限纳入仓库，避免 release
构建修改工作树。

## Testing
- 未跑自动化测试；本次只调整 Git 文件模式和 workflow 步骤。
- 已本地确认变更仅包含 workflow 删除 `chmod` 与脚本权限位改为 `100755`。

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: A syntax-highlighting cat built around Tree-sitter.

commit.message:
Honor hidden options in duplicate checks

Hidden options still participate in parsing, so constructor-time
duplicate option validation must treat them as syntax-bearing terms
instead of filtering them out as purely user-facing metadata.

Add regression tests for hidden-visible and hidden-hidden collisions in
object(), tuple(), and merge(), route duplicate detection through an
internal helper that includes fully hidden option names, and update the
docs and changelog to describe the construction-time behavior.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/510

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: optique
repository.description: Type-safe combinatorial CLI parser for TypeScript

commit.message:
refactor(chat): remove lingering frontend budget suppressions (#674)

Summary:
- extract Project AI terminal connection logic into dedicated helpers and
  shared types so terminal lifecycle behavior no longer needs inline
  eslint budget suppressions
- drop the stale max-lines suppression from the workspace browser detail
  panel and flatten chat/settings tests that only used describe-level
  bypasses
- keep the cleanup scoped to the existing chat workspace and provider
  settings behavior while preserving current coverage

Rationale:
- ASE-172 asks for temporary frontend file-length bypasses to be replaced
  with maintainable implementations rather than more allowlists
- moving the websocket/session lifecycle into focused helpers makes the
  terminal manager easier to evolve without hiding debt behind disables
- removing stale suppressions keeps the structure rules honest and lets
  remaining warnings surface naturally instead of being silently ignored

Tests:
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/chat/terminal-manager.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-workspace-browser-detail.test.ts src/lib/features/settings/components/agent-settings.test.ts
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run lint:structure
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run lint:deps

- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run ci

commit.message:
fix(slack): guide participant mentions through prompt context (#177)

Guide Slack mentions for known thread participants through prompt
context instead of rewriting outbound message text.

The earlier branch direction added a post-send mention resolver plus
workspace-wide Slack user lookups. That cut across the normal streaming
reply path, risked rewriting non-mention text like links and package
names, and added avoidable Slack API load. This version removes that
machinery and keeps the change at the existing identity-context
boundary.

We now inject thread participants into the system prompt with the exact
`<@USERID>` token the model should use when referring to someone already
in the conversation. Slack output formatting stays synchronous and
unchanged, which preserves the repo's standard streamed
`AsyncIterable<string>` reply path.

Integration coverage now protects the important behavior boundary by
asserting streamed Slack output is posted unchanged, while the unit
coverage stays focused on local output normalization.

Closes #174
Closes #175

---------

Co-authored-by: junior <junior@sentry.io>
Co-authored-by: David Cramer <dcramer@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(evolution): add governed improvement benchmark proof

Summary:
- add the governed_improvement benchmark module, richer benchmark-gate policy, and operator benchmark surfaces for anti-misevolution, canary, rollback, and receipt visibility
- expand runtime evals, backend tests, cockpit coverage, and shipped-truth docs for the governed improvement batch
- tighten the operator contract to report recent saved proposal receipts truthfully and degrade summary status when governed benchmark regressions exist

Rationale:
- Batch BM needs deterministic proof that self-improvement stays governed, review-gated, and rollback-ready rather than drifting silently
- the operator surface has to show only what the system can actually prove from persisted receipts, not blocked validations that were never saved

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/operator.py backend/src/evals/benchmark_catalog.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/src/evolution/engine.py backend/src/evolution/benchmark.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py backend/tests/test_evolution_api.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_evolution_api.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -o addopts=''
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

commit.message:
refactor(settings): streamline panel and make search navigation actionable

Summary:
- Removed the interface-direction mode switcher from Settings and cleaned
  related state/imports.
- Made search overlay results interactive so selecting a result jumps to
  the relevant settings tab and closes the search panel.
- Converted settings tabs to controlled state to support result-driven
  tab navigation while preserving existing tab content.

Rationale:
- Product direction is to avoid exposing frontend mode toggles in this
  panel and instead improve the existing settings flow itself.
- Search already indexed the right metadata but stopped at static text;
  making results actionable improves speed without adding UI noise.

Tests:
- npm run lint -- app/settings/page.tsx

commit.message:
feat: streamline admin kyc and shipment flows (#1174)

* fix: streamline CAC registration search

* feat: align analytics across web and mobile

* feat: polish analytics currency and transaction flows

* fix(mobile-admin): address coderabbit analytics polish findings

- stabilize [metric].tsx fallback date range via useState initializer so
  useAnalyticsDetail's query key no longer churns per render
- drop stale "pull to refresh" wording from analytics insights error state
  and simplify its row map key (row.id already encodes kind+index)
- in transactions, guard orders.map behind !error so stale data doesn't
  render behind the error state, add disabled visual cue to the Cancel
  pressable, and pull the save-button foreground from colors.textOnPrimary
  instead of hardcoded #FFFFFF
- replace the double type assertion in ReportSelectionModal with a typed
  TaxLedgerOrderRow and mapRowToTransaction helper that normalizes the
  joined customer shape
- cache Intl currency-symbol lookups in lib/utils, handle negative amounts
  correctly in formatCompactCurrency (-₦1.50M), and use locale-aware
  formatting in the billion/million branches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(analytics): address second-round coderabbit findings

Mobile-admin:
- stabilize insights.tsx fallback timestamp via useState initializer so
  useAnalyticsOverview's query key stops churning per render
- guard useAnalyticsDetail against invalid parsed dates before hitting
  toISOString; reorder UseAnalyticsDetailOptions for readability
- useTransactionReview uses the @/hooks/useMerchant alias and rejects
  queries when merchant.id is missing
- useTopSellingProducts aligns queryKey and queryFn on shared
  ALL_TIME_START_ISO / ALL_TIME_END_ISO sentinels
- extract DEFAULT_FILTER_LABEL constant, widen useLocalSearchParams
  metric to optional, replace remaining hardcoded style numbers in
  transactions.tsx with RADIUS/SPACING tokens, add accessibility
  attributes to the item row Pressable
- insights.tsx simplifies the row map key to row.id and drops the stale
  "please try again" copy
- analytics-period swaps inverted custom date ranges and documents the
  intentional selectedYear semantics for this_year/last_year
- analytics-period.test.ts covers inverted ranges and full endDate UTC
  time boundary
- ReportsGenerator guards topProducts optional chaining and tax-rate
  division-by-zero
- formatCompactCurrency uses Intl compact notation for locale-aware
  symbol placement, with a manual fallback for older runtimes

Web backend:
- get-merchant-analytics-overview sanitizes customer name/email fields
  through sanitizeText before returning them in the API response
- buildCustomerBreakdown keys on customer_id/email so two customers
  with the same display name no longer collide
- collapse anonymous guest checkouts into a single "guest" bucket when
  counting unique customers
- fix previousStart duration math so the comparison window matches the
  current window exactly
- switch buildChartData to UTC date arithmetic + UTC toLocaleDateString
  so bucket keys are stable across timezones
- quantity ?? 1 instead of quantity || 1 so legitimate zero quantities
  don't inflate units or profit
- getPercentChange branches on previous === 0 only
- document intentional non-paid filters on currentOrders and
  activeOrdersResult
- draggable-analytics-grid comments the intentional inverted Tax Due
  trend (lower tax = positive for cash flow)
- route.test.ts asserts exact Date values and a non-empty error string
  on the 400 response

Shared types:
- extract MerchantBlogTopPost interface
- MerchantAnalyticsNamedValue extends MerchantAnalyticsBreakdownItem to
  remove near-duplicate fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(analytics): address third-round coderabbit findings

* feat: harden analytics and transaction reporting

* fix: tighten analytics feedback and typing

* Fix analytics bucket timezone and widget naming

* Fix analytics detail bucket imports and immutability

* fix: address analytics review follow-ups

* fix: harden analytics and CAC review flows

* test: stabilize security form timeout

* fix: address analytics review nits

* refactor: clean up analytics mobile hooks

* test: cover currency hook and modal keyboard layout

* feat: streamline admin kyc and shipment flows

* fix: address PR #1174 review findings

* fix: address PR #1174 review findings

* fix: polish PR #1174 follow-up review notes

* fix: satisfy PR #1174 guardrails and lint

* test: align PR #1174 mobile-admin CI coverage

* fix: unblock PR #1174 mobile-admin typecheck

* fix: close PR #1174 remaining review gaps

* test: fix PR #1174 mobile admin CI regressions

* test: align PR #1174 assertions with runtime behavior

* fix: restore mobile admin test types in CI

* fix: avoid zod resolver type mismatch in mobile admin

* test: stabilize self-fulfill schema assertions

commit.message:
fix: address PR#194 review comments

1. requirements.py & architect.py: make milestone inner function async and
   use asyncio.iscoroutine() to await only when emit_milestone is async.
   All 8/13 call sites now awaited.

2. generation_service.py _update_generation_agent: clear progress_text on
   terminal transitions so UI doesn't show stale running text.

3. generation_service.py emit_generation_agent_event: move downstream
   unblocking before broadcasts so clients see corrected downstream state
   in the same snapshot.

4. architect.py: emit waiting_for_first_event immediately after started
   instead of only on first node/edge, so long LLM pauses show feedback.

5. useCanvasPipeline.ts: add generationStartedAt to ticker effect deps
   so interval starts when first event sets the state. Parse
   event.started_at ISO string to epoch ms instead of using Date.now()
   for clock-skew accuracy and correct reconnect behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Release v2026.7.0

- Add generation_agent_event handler to useCanvasPipeline.ts

  timer semantics, two-message pattern explanation

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(backend): instrument agent milestones for event-driven observability

Chunk 2: Add real milestone emissions to Requirements, Architect, and Cost Analyst agents.

Requirements agent:
- Add emit_milestone callback parameter to generate_requirements()
- Emit requesting_model, parsing_output, validating_requirements,
  repairing_output, applying_budget_rules, completed, failed milestones

Architect agent:
- Add emit_milestone callback parameter to stream_architecture()
- Emit bucketed milestones: started, waiting_for_first_event,
  network_layout_started, service_layout_started, connections_started,
  parse_warning, stall_warning, completed
- Do NOT emit per-node milestones

Cost Analyst agent:
- Emit started, choosing_region, inventorying_services, pricing_services,
  applying_usage_profile, calculating_totals, completed, skipped milestones
- Use runtime.emit_generation_agent_event() directly

generation_service.py:
- Wire emit_milestone in _generate_requirements_with_retry()
- Wire emit_milestone in stream_architecture call
- cost_analyst calls runtime.emit_generation_agent_event directly

commit.message:
feat(backend): add emit_generation_agent_event for event-driven observability

Chunk 1 Task 1: Replace hardcoded copy maps with event-driven state.

- Add GenerationRuntime.emit_generation_agent_event() method that:
  - Accepts agent, status, event_type, message, history, error
  - Updates summary, progress_text, history, started_at, completed_at, elapsed_ms
  - Broadcasts both generation_agent_event and generation_agent_update
  - Handles downstream blocked/queued transitions on terminal events
- Remove static _AGENT_HISTORY, _AGENT_RUNNING_SUMMARY, _AGENT_COMPLETED_SUMMARY maps
- Update _update_generation_agent() to not use static maps
- Update test _FakeRuntime classes to support init_generation_observability

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(guardian): ship user-model restraint batch

Summary:
- add the guardian user-model benchmark suite, ambiguity-aware restraint state, and operator benchmark proof surfaces
- surface user-model receipts in the cockpit and extend deterministic backend/frontend coverage for the BI batch

Rationale:
- Batch BI needs one aggregate batch PR that makes user modeling, clarification, and restraint explicit rather than implicit
- review found overstated benchmark proof, low-urgency abstention drift, and mismatched facet evidence, so the batch now fixes those before PR

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/guardian/benchmark.py backend/src/guardian/state.py backend/src/guardian/world_model.py backend/tests/test_guardian_benchmark.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_guardian_benchmark.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -o addopts=''
- cd frontend && npm test -- --run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

canvas now enters single-line edit mode.

widget coverage for both whole-line and line-segment double clicks,
and document the change in the unreleased changelog.
